work.name: Sberbank
work.summary:
Development and implementation of NLP solutions

1. Development of an AI assistant to help clients
- Created a chatbot that classifies a user request and is able to process questions about the department's products using Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) (Multivector Retriever).
- Developed the retriever quality control functionality.
- Implemented functionality for charting exchange rates and metals on request, providing indicative quotes and recommendations on the company's products.
- Developed a module for displaying news for the day.
- Developed autotests to ensure stable operation of the chatbot
stack: Langchain, GigaChat, Weaviate, Playwright

2. Development of a chatbot for market analysis
- Developed a chatbot to provide market research analytics, charting and analyzing changes in product prices.
- Implemented RAG to handle requests related to changes in the forecasts of the reserch.

3. A model for summarizing and clustering client requests
- Created a model for automatic summarization of customer requests in different formats with further classification by product and subject.
- Implemented clustering algorithms for unclassified requests.
stack: BERTopic, UMAP, HDBSCAN

4. Finetuning LLM for sentiment analysis
- Carried out fine-tuning of LLM using LoRA for the task of analyzing the sentiment of Central Bank meetings.

5. Automatic summary of economic news for the Telegram channel
- Participated in the development of a model for the daily posting of a brief economic summary for the day in the Telegram channel.
- Was responsible for the development of a module for data processing, parsing of exchange rates, raw materials, information on stocks, bonds, stock indexes.
stack: Parsel, httpx, pandas

6. Classification of client messages and tonality analysis
- Developed a model for classifying customer messages into specified classes and determining tonality using BERT.

education.institution: Russian State University for the Humanities

publications.name: Zur Elektrody/namik bewegter Körper
publications.summary: It concerned an interpretation of the Michelson–Morley experiment and the properties of light and time. Special relativity incorporates the principle that the speed of light is the same for all inertial observers regardless of the state of motion of the source.

publications.name: Über einen die Erzeugung und Verwandlung des Lichtes betreffenden heuristischen Gesichtspunkt
publications.summary: In the second paper, he applied the quantum theory to light to explain the photoelectric effect. In particular, he used the idea of light quanta (photons) to explain experimental results, but stressed the importance of the experimental results. The importance of his work on the photoelectric effect earned him the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1921.

publications.name: Die Grundlage der allgemeinen Relativitätstheorie
publications.summary: The publication of the theory of general relativity made him internationally famous. He was professor of physics at the universities of Zurich (1909–1911) and Prague (1911–1912), before he returned to ETH Zurich (1912–1914).

projects.name: Quantum Computing
projects.summary: Quantum computing is the use of quantum-mechanical phenomena such as superposition and entanglement to perform computation. Computers that perform quantum computations are known as quantum computers.
